signals.signal_title: Sunoco LP acquires NuStar Energy — active IT integration of fuel distribution and pipeline systems
signals.signal_description: Sunoco LP completed its $7.3B acquisition of NuStar Energy in May 2024, adding ~9,500 miles of pipeline and 63 terminal facilities. Post-acquisition IT integration of fuel distribution, terminal management, pipeline control, and ERP systems creates near-term IT spend demand. Systems consolidation across two formerly separate companies is a multi-year effort.

enrichment_notes: FY2024 revenue $22.69B — fuel distribution MLP (Energy Transfer subsidiary), nearly all commodity pass-through. Sub-sector = Downstream Refining (fuel distribution, not manufacturing). Denominator = Gross Profit or EBITDA × 1.5 or Revenue × 0.06. ~6,000 employees post-NuStar acquisition. Dallas HQ in STOLA — clean account. Energy Transfer is general partner and controlling LP. CEO Joseph Kim confirmed. No CIO identified. No MSP, CoE, or AI programs. $7.3B NuStar acquisition (May 2024) creates IT integration demand. WWT opportunity: Dallas STOLA account, NuStar IT integration demand, but Energy Transfer parent relationship may drive IT decisions centrally.
